summarization.a: The figure displays the energy profiles for H₂ and N₂ chemisorption on three different TiN crystal surfaces: TiN (001), N-terminated TiN (111), and Ti-terminated TiN (111). For each surface, the energy diagrams show the initial state (IS), transition state (TS), and final state (FS) energies for both H₂ and N₂ chemisorption. Notably, H₂ chemisorption is energetically favorable on all three surfaces, with the lowest activation energy on the N-terminated TiN (111) surface (0.26 eV). In contrast, N₂ chemisorption is only favorable on the Ti-terminated TiN (111) surface, where it proceeds through a moderate energy barrier. These results highlight the strong dependence of surface reactivity on crystal orientation and termination.
